title: Request-Response Cycle
summary: The web works as a simple back and forth.
The client sends a request asking for something.
The server sends a response with the answer.
This ask and receive pattern repeats for everything you load.

sections.text: Almost everything you do online is one simple pattern repeated over and over. Ask, receive, ask again. Once you see the pattern, the whole web starts to make sense.

sections.title: What is a Request?
sections.text: A request is the message your browser sends to a server. It says what you want, like a page or an image, and where to find it. Every click and every page load starts with a request.

sections.title: What is a Response?
sections.text: A response is the server's reply. It carries what you asked for, like the page content, plus a short status that says whether things went well or something went wrong.

media.alt: A request arrow going out and a response arrow coming back

sections.title: A Quick Conversation
sections.text: The cycle is like asking a friend a question and getting an answer. You ask, they reply. If you have another question, you ask again. The web is just this conversation happening very fast, many times over.

media.alt: Two people in a quick back and forth conversation

sections.title: The Request and Response Cycle
items: You do something, like click a link or load a page.
Your browser sends a request to the server.
The server reads the request and prepares what was asked for.
The server sends a response back.
Your browser shows the result and waits for your next action.

sections.text: A single web page can trigger dozens of request and response pairs at once, one for the page and more for each image, font, and script it needs.

terms.definition: The message a browser sends asking for something.
The server's reply, carrying what was asked for.
The repeating back and forth of request then response.
A short code that says if the response went well or not.
The device making the request, like your browser.

options: The message the browser sends asking for something
A picture on a web page
questions.explanation: A request is the message the browser sends to a server to ask for a page, image, or data.

options: What you asked for, plus a short status
questions.explanation: A response carries what was asked for along with a status that says whether it went well.

questions.text: What is the repeating pattern of the web called?
questions.explanation: The web repeats a request then a response, over and over. That is the request and response cycle.
